title: Genome mining unveils a class of ribosomal peptides with two amino termini
objective: Discover and characterize a new RiPP class using class-independent genome mining approach based on RiPP Recognition Element (RRE) domains, revealing a RiPP class with unprecedented two amino termini defined by a unique post-translational modification
problem_statement: Despite success in RiPP discovery, genome mining of new RiPP classes remains unsolved. Traditional class-dependent genome mining approaches are limited to known enzyme families. Nearly all founding members of RiPP classes were identified by bioassay-guided screening. A new class-independent approach is needed to discover RiPPs with entirely novel post-translational modifications and structures

discovery_methodology.approach: Class-independent genome mining using RiPP Recognition Element (RRE) domains
discovery_methodology.rationale: RRE domains are the most common domains involved in RiPP biosynthesis (found in 19 of 41 known prokaryotic RiPP classes)
workflow: Identified all RRE-containing proteins from databases
Analyzed co-occurrence of RRE domains with nearby ORFs
Sorted by sequence similarity into RRE families
Analyzed for highly similar co-occurring ORFs
Generated sequence logos to identify conserved motifs
Expanded predictions using PSI-BLAST and pHMM

daptide_definition.name: Daptides
daptide_definition.type: Ribosomally synthesized and post-translationally modified peptides (RiPPs)
daptide_definition.key_characteristic: Harbor two positively charged termini (N-terminus and C-terminus)

genomic_prevalence.bgc_identified: Nearly 500 daptide biosynthetic gene clusters identified

precursor_peptide_structure.description: RRE-peptidase fusion protein with recognition sequence

components.core_region: C-terminal region containing branched-chain aliphatic amino acids and invariant C-terminal Thr

precursor_peptide_structure.recognition_motif: ELExMEAP at N-terminal of core region

modification_sequence.action: Oxidative decarboxylation of C-terminal threonine residue

biosynthetic_pathway.substrate_specificity: Oxidative decarboxylase tolerates variability in core region while being leader peptide-dependent

conserved_genes.description: Domain of unknown function fused to RRE domain

bioactivity_basis: Net positive charge on both termini
biological_activity.suspected_mechanism: Membrane targeting and disruption aided by dual positive charges

significance.discovery_impact: Showcases class-independent genome mining for discovering RiPP biosynthetic enzyme families
significance.novel_chemistry: Provides first example of ribosomal peptides with two amino termini
significance.methodology_impact: Demonstrates RRE-based approach for discovering entirely new RiPP classes with novel PTMs
significance.future_applications: Logical route for discovering additional ribosomal peptides with unconventional terminal modifications

ripp_class_context.daptide_novelty: First completely new RiPP class discovered using RRE-based genome mining
